_comment: Key illustrated events for Chapter 9 — the oath at Raireshwar. The oath ceremony itself is cherished TRADITION (labelled); Shivaji's documented commitment to Swarajya in these years anchors it. 'anchor' is an EXACT substring of chapters/ch09.md. 'refs' are entity slugs from reference/index.json. 'image' filled by tools/refgen.py AFTER references are signed off.

events.title: Before dawn
events.anchor: They climbed to the temple before dawn
events.scene_prompt: Before dawn in the Sahyadri mountains: a small band of young men — teenage Shivaji and his closest Maval companions (the big Tanaji Malusare, the wiry Yesaji Kank, other hill youths) — climb a steep mountain path toward a high temple, breath smoking in the cold, the black hills just beginning to brighten against a pre-dawn sky. Hushed, purposeful, secret. Shivaji has his thin moustache and no beard, matching his reference.

events.title: The temple on the plateau
events.anchor: stood on its high plateau the way it had stood for centuries
events.scene_prompt: Wide establishing shot, NO crowd of people in the foreground: the ancient Raireshwar Shiva temple standing alone on its vast windswept high plateau in the Sahyadri mountains, matching the temple reference exactly — a small, weathered, austere DARK-STONE Hemadpanthi temple with strong square stone pillars and a modest stone shikhara (tower), dwarfed by the huge mountain sky and the rolling ranges beyond. Soft natural early daylight with balanced cool-and-neutral colour (NOT an orange or golden glow). Quiet, sacred, timeless — the deliberate opposite of a golden court.

events.title: Tonight we stop talking around it
events.anchor: The lamp threw their shadows huge on the walls
events.scene_prompt: Inside the dark stone Raireshwar temple, lit by a single small oil lamp: teenage Shivaji stands and speaks quietly but with fierce resolve to his small circle of Maval companions (Tanaji, Yesaji and others), their shadows thrown huge on the ancient stone walls behind them. A traditional Shiva lingam — a smooth, UPRIGHT, clearly PROTRUDING tall rounded black-stone shaft on a circular stone yoni base with a small spout — sits apart on its altar to one side, and the men keep a RESPECTFUL DISTANCE from it, gathered around Shivaji rather than crowding the deity. Every companion, including the big Tanaji Malusare, is SOLEMN and serious here (no grinning), expressions befitting the grave occasion. Intense, intimate, charged with purpose. Shivaji has his thin moustache and no beard, matching his reference.

events.title: The oath of Swarajya (the story goes)
events.anchor: his own blood, a few drops of it, pressed to the cold stone
events.scene_prompt: The cherished legend, told reverently: in the lamplit dark of the ancient Raireshwar Shiva temple, teenage Shivaji kneels before a traditional Shiva lingam — a smooth, UPRIGHT, clearly PROTRUDING tall rounded black-stone shaft rising from a circular stone yoni base with a small spout (gomukh) — and makes his solemn vow to win Swarajya, sealing it by pressing a few drops of his own blood to the stone, his devoted Maval companions gathered behind him in the flickering light. EVERY companion — including the big Tanaji Malusare — is SOLEMN, grave and reverent here (absolutely NO grinning or smiling), faces befitting a sacred oath. Sacred, emotional, the spiritual heart of the whole story. Handled gently — solemn, not gory. Shivaji has his thin moustache and no beard, matching his reference.

events.title: The hills turning gold
events.anchor: the sun was coming up over the edge of the world
events.scene_prompt: Sunrise: teenage Shivaji and his Maval companions (Tanaji, Yesaji, the older deshmukh Baji Pasalkar, and others) step out of the small stone temple onto the high plateau as the sun rises over the edge of the world and the endless Sahyadri hills turn gold. Quiet, uplifted, transformed — a vow freshly sworn, an impossible hope made real. Warm dawn light, vast hopeful horizon.
